prompt:
The snapshot query tool has been fine for months — the on-call folks use it constantly with `-Name` and `-State` lookups. This week we pointed the dashboard cron at it and BOTH of the cron's call shapes blow up before the script even runs:

    $ pwsh -NoProfile -File ./svcsnap.ps1 -Snapshot day.json
    svcsnap.ps1: Parameter set cannot be resolved using the specified named parameters. One or more parameters issued cannot be used together or an insufficient number of parameters were provided.

    $ pwsh -NoProfile -File ./svcsnap.ps1 -Snapshot day.json -State failed -Limit 5
    svcsnap.ps1: Parameter set cannot be resolved using the specified named parameters. One or more parameters issued cannot be used together or an insufficient number of parameters were provided.

Which is baffling because the exact same file works fine interactively: `-Name db-main` works, `-State failed` works (until you add `-Limit`), and `-Name db-main -Limit 5` works too. So it's not the snapshot, and it's not the filtering logic — plain listing doesn't even involve a filter.

What the cron needs is exactly what the tool's own header comment promises: run it with just `-Snapshot` and get the whole snapshot listed; add `-Limit` to cap any query; `-CountOnly` with just `-Snapshot` should count everything (that one's broken the same way, by the way).

One thing that must NOT change while fixing this: `-Name` and `-State` together are meant to be rejected — they're deliberately exclusive, and the ops wiki documents that. Don't 'fix' the error by making the tool quietly accept both.

Fix svcsnap.ps1 and prove it with `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_svcsnap.ps1` — the test file stays as it is.
notes: 6P1 item 6: parameter-set ambiguity debug. Two related bugs, both in attribute metadata, zero broken lines of logic: (a) no DefaultParameterSetName on [CmdletBinding()], so a call binding only the common -Snapshot cannot resolve a set; (b) -Limit carries ParameterSetName='ByName' (it sits grouped under -Name where it was presumably added for top-N name search), so -State + -Limit spans two sets and cannot resolve either. Probed 2026-07-16 on pwsh 7.6.3: both shapes emit 'Parameter set cannot be resolved using the specified named parameters.' exit 1; with DefaultParameterSetName present and Limit set-free, bare/-Limit/-CountOnly calls resolve and -Name+-State still errors (pinned by the 'exclusive' case so the fix cannot be deleting the sets wholesale). Body already handles all shapes via $PSBoundParameters checks, so the reference fix touches only the param block. Prompt is symptom-first with the pasted engine error (the symptom), never names sets/attributes/the guilty parameter. Deterministic: re-ran the failing shapes 5x, identical output. Domain (collector snapshot query, honest cross-platform shape per wave-6 conventions) overlaps no existing seed; go-statuspoll is a polling loop, different layer.
